ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Mediation
Off Calendar Jul-29-2014 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christine Gallagher Nelson for Krista Hansen and Elise Beaman and making a courtesy appearance for attorney John Hartog for William Meckel. Ronald Richman for (respondent) Justin MacNeil.

Mr. Richman reported that they settled and that they will be presenting a stipulation to redact confidential information on tax returns and a stipulation for dismissal and exoneration of bond and for the court to reserve jurisdiction pursuant to CCP 664.6. Status of mediation, the petition for instructions and the accounting petition will go off calendar subject to review of the stipulations and proposed orders.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy M. Jones. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-24-2014 Continued To Jul-28-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-24-2014 Continued To Jul-28-14 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: John Hartog for and with William Meckel. Christina Gallagher Nelson for and with Elsie Beaman and for Krista Hanson (Beneficiaries). Seth Skootsky and Ronald Richman for and with Justin Macniel (Trustee).

Mr. Skootsky stated that they had two preliminary meetings, that mediation is scheduled for June 18th and that June 25th is set aside for a further session. Mr. Hartog stated that it is important that duplication of expenses be stopped and that Ms. Macniel should pay for Mr. Skootsky's expenses out of her own pocket. Mr. Skootksy explained that multiple pleadings were prepared by Mr. Richman's firm and paid by the insurance company. Mr. Richman stated that the Professional Liability Defense Policy needs to be looked at, that the policy does not include trust administration, and that the policy clearly defines what is covered and what isn't. Ms. Nelson stated that her Client has spent $100,000.00, that her Client is aligned with Mr. Hartog's position and that the Trustee has spent almost $1,000,000.00 in Attorney fees. The Petition for Instructions and the accounting petition were continued to July 28, 2014 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Status on mediation was set for that same date. The Petition for Instructions Compelling Trustee to Cease Spending Trust Funds, etc. was taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Court Examiner: Sandy Hilton. Court Reporter: Dawn Stark, CSR# 7847, (415) 692-4945. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy J. Leonardini. (Reported)

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Aug-19-2013 Continued To Sep-30-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ronald Richman and Seth Skootsky for Justin Macneil. Christina Gallagher for and with Elise Beaman and for Krista Hanson. Jonna Thomas for William Meckel (Beneficiary).

Ms. Thomas stated that they have received many documents, that they are still waiting for more documents, that they are still waiting for distributions that were represented by Mr. Skootsky to be made and that conditions are now changing. Ms. Thomas also stated that the Beneficiaries are no longer in a position to sign waivers of liability and release, that she is in the process of preparing motions for immediate disribution of assets and that at this point the Beneficiaries are at an empass. Ms. Gallagher expressed her Client's frustration and suggested mediation for global resolution. Mr. Skootksy stated that there is a tax issue. Ms. Thomas stated that she would like to arrange for mediation within 90-days. The Petition for Instructions and the Accounting Petition were continued to September 30, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for further status.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Loos. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-20-2013 Continued To Jun-10-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Apr-29-2013 Continued To Jun-03-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christina Nelson for Krista Hanson and for Elise Beaman. Seth Skootsky for and with Justin MacNeil. Jonna Thomas for and with William Meckel.

Counsel informed the Court that the proposed order regarding the March 18, 2013 hearing has been reviewed by all Counsel and that the form of order is still in dispute. Ms. Nelson and Ms. Thomas object to Mr. Skootsky submitting an ex parte petition regarding bond. Ms. Nelson explained that they have received some documents, that the excel accounting sheet was altered, that they received a second estate tax return and a draft of a K1 on April 15,2013 for the beneficiaries to file. Mr. Skootsky explained circumstances as to why documents were provided in that fashion. The Court directs all counsel to meet and confer regarding language of the proposed order and the bond issue. The Court also urged Mr. Skootsky to provide all information requested in a timely manner. The petition for instruction and the accounting petition were continued to June 03, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status on discovery and status on negotiations.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Clerk: Nancy Bautista/Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Apr-29-2013 Continued To Jun-03-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-18-2013 Continued To Apr-29-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christina Nelson for and with Krista Hanson and for Elise Beaman. Seth Skootsky for and with Justin MacNeil. Jonna Thomas for William Meckel.

The Court noted that discovery needs to be conducted, stated that it was not ruling on whether the Trustee should be removed without an evidentary hearing, that today's issue is regarding suspension of the Trustee and that it's tentative ruling is that the Court does not have enough evidence to suspend the Trustee's powers. The Court also informed Counsel that late filings will not be considered. Counsel presented oral argument to support their positions with regard to suspension of powers of Ms. MacNeil as Trustee. Ms. Nelson and Ms. Thomas requested that Interim Co-Trustees be appointed. Mr. Skootsky introduced Randy Sugarman (CPA), Michael Helms (CPA), and Thomas Worth (Estate Tax Attorney), and pointed out that Ms. McNeil has distinguished advisors assisiting her in the administration of this trust. The Court advised counsel to step out of the courtroom to discuss the issue of bond. Upon returning, the parties agreed on posting of a $5,000,000.00 bond, indicated to the Court that the bond agent needed four weeks to prepare the bond documentation. Ms. Nelson stated that they agreed to do informal discovery in efforts to reach a global settlement, that she will prepare a list of requested information and that she needs to receive the information within one week. The Court will not suspend the Trustee's powers, stated that the Trustee has full powers and set bond at $5,000,000.00. The Petition for Instructions and the Accounting Petition were continued to April 29, 2013 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for status of discovery and status of posting of bond. Mr. Skootsky is to prepare an order reflecting the above.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Court Reporter: Tamara Willat-Harrison CSR# 4609 (415) 272-1753 Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-18-2013 Continued To Apr-29-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Mar-11-2013 Continued To Mar-18-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Christina Nelson for and with Krista Hanson and for Elise Beaman. Seth Skootsky for and with Justin MacNeil. Jonna Thomas for William Meckel.

Mr. Skootsky addressed the issues raised in the Examiner's notes with regard to filing his response on February 6, 2013 instead of the court ordered date of February 4, 2013, and stated that he did not have the opportunity to repond to all of the allegations. The Court addressed the issue of the response not being noticed to all the beneficiaries and advised Mr. Skootsky that notice should be given to all parties for any future filings. Ms. Thomas stated that there is a $12 million loss to the Trust Estate due to the Trustee's mishandlings and requested that the Court remove the Trustee or in the least suspend the Trustee's powers. Ms. Nelson explained circumstances (taxes, accountings, property not put into the LLC) to support her position with regard to removal of Ms. MacNeil. The Court orders in open court that Ms. MacNeil is prohibited to make any payment of fees to herself, relatives or any other professional for one week. The Petition for Instructions and the Accounting Petition were continued to March 18, 2013 at 10:30 a.m., in Dept. 204 for the Court to address the issue of suspension.

Judge: Lillian K. Sing. Court Reporter: Mary Richardson, CSR #13143 (510)769-6131. Clerk: Rosie Noguera/ Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy J. Chen.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Mar-11-2013 Continued To Mar-18-13 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 204

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Dec-03-2012 Continued To Mar-11-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Seth Skootsky for Justin MacNeil (Trustee). Jonna Thomas for and with William Meckel. Christina Gallagher Nelson for and with Elsie Beaman and for Crista Hansen (Beneficiaries).

Ms. Nelson stated that the chances of resolving their disputes are slim to none. Ms. Thomas stated she is in the same position, that they have made numerous requests for information, that the requests came back with minimul information, that the accountings are late since 2007 and that they never had a complete accounting. In addition, Ms. Thomas stated that the Trust continues to lose assets, and that all four Beneficiaries agree to the need to modify the Trust and remove Justin MacNeil as Trustee. Ms. Nelson also stated that she wants a fixed date for an inventory, an accounting from 2007 through current, the federal Estate taxes (financials) and Justin MacNeil removed as Trustee. Mr. Skootsky presented oral argument to support his position. The Petition for Instrcutions was continued to March 11, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The accounting (2007 through 2012) is to be filed by February 4, 2013. Responses to the accounting are due by March 5, 2013. Mr. Skootsky is to provide the financial information as requested above. The Court will not order removal of Justin MacNeil at this time.

Judge: Paul Alvarado. Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy Benton.

PETITION FOR INSTRUCTIONS
Off Calendar Oct-26-2012 Continued To Dec-03-12 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
